apps/auth/src/lib/local-dev-jwt.ts:75 — getLocalDevTokenEmail verifies the signature but not exp, so refreshAppTokensInternal can mint fresh tokens from an expired signed local-dev refresh token. Validate expiration and add an expired-token test.

I’ll tighten the local-dev JWT validation path and add a focused regression test for expired refresh tokens. First I’ll read the auth docs and the relevant token/test files so the change matches the app’s existing contract.

**Updating authentication and TypeScript**

I realize I need to modify and inspect some components. It seems like I must update certain aspects, and my first step is to add commentary. Maybe I should check the authentication documentation since there are non-trivial changes to understand. I’ll focus on the relevant details there and ensure I’m correctly incorporating TypeScript. Additionally, I’ll choose the right tools for this task. Time to get organized!

The docs already describe stale local-dev refresh cookies being cleared, so the code should reject signed-but-expired local-dev refresh tokens instead of using their email. I’m checking the existing tests now and will add the regression where the helper currently accepts an expired token.

I found the vulnerable helper: it verifies the HMAC and standard local-dev claims, then returns the email without checking `exp`. I’m going to keep the fix inside that helper so both direct refresh-token fallback and auth/id token fallback share the same expiration behavior.

content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/auth/src/lib/local-dev-jwt.ts
@@
   if (
     payload?.iss !== LOCAL_DEV_ISSUER ||
     payload.client_id !== LOCAL_DEV_CLIENT_ID ||
     !isLocalDevTokenUse(payload.token_use) ||
+    typeof payload.exp !== "number" ||
+    payload.exp <= Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) ||
     typeof payload.email !== "string" ||
     !payload.email.trim()
   ) {
*** End Patch

The code now treats missing or expired `exp` as invalid before returning the email, and the new test signs an already-expired refresh-shaped token to cover the reported path. I’ll run the focused auth test next.

Added a regression test in `apps/auth/src/lib/local-dev-jwt.test.ts` for an expired signed local-dev refresh token.

Verified with:

`mise exec -- pnpm --filter auth test src/lib/local-dev-jwt.test.ts`
